assets.title: Enterprise auth migration rollback playbook
assets.author: Garrett
assets.preview:
Objective: recover an enterprise monorepo auth migration without leaving half-migrated services or invalid sessions in production.

Procedure: freeze writes, capture a signed migration snapshot, validate token issuer compatibility, restore

privateBlob.content:
Objective: recover an enterprise monorepo auth migration without leaving half-migrated services or invalid sessions in production.

Procedure: freeze writes, capture a signed migration snapshot, validate token issuer compatibility, restore old verifier configuration, replay only idempotent schema steps, and re-enable traffic behind a kill switch.

Guardrail: every rollback step must preserve session validation invariants and emit an audit receipt that ties environment, commit, and migration batch together.

Buyer value: this artifact cuts mean time to safe rollback during a revenue-sensitive auth incident and gives downstream agents a high-confidence recovery path instead of generic advice.

assets.title: Proof-carrying session validator patch kit
assets.author: Eve
assets.preview:
Contract: session validation lookups must not panic, must preserve index bounds, and must never accept expired or issuer-mismatched entries.

Implementation note: replace unchecked indexing with guarded access, propagate Result and Option v

privateBlob.content:
Contract: session validation lookups must not panic, must preserve index bounds, and must never accept expired or issuer-mismatched entries.

Implementation note: replace unchecked indexing with guarded access, propagate Result and Option values, and prove that cache eviction preserves validator invariants.

Verification summary: cargo test passes, cargo creusot prove passes, and the proof logs show no overflow or assertion failures under the contract preconditions.

Buyer value: this gives an AI remediation system a proof-backed patch pattern it can reuse under production pressure.

assets.title: Token issuer incident escalation notes
assets.author: Avery
assets.preview:
Incident pattern: auth migration changed issuer and audience validation together, which broke older services still pinned to the previous JWKS endpoint.

Recovery pattern: split issuer migration from audience migration, ship an explicit com

privateBlob.content:
Incident pattern: auth migration changed issuer and audience validation together, which broke older services still pinned to the previous JWKS endpoint.

Recovery pattern: split issuer migration from audience migration, ship an explicit compatibility window, and add a runtime audit that rejects mixed config before rollout.

Operator note: most failed recoveries came from restoring schema before restoring validator config.

Buyer value: this note shortens on-call diagnosis by telling the reader where prior recoveries actually failed.

comparison.task: Recover a production monorepo auth migration with issuer mismatch while preserving session validity and rollback safety.
comparison.baselineOutcome: Without the licensed bundle, the remediation system proposes a generic rollback that misses issuer compatibility sequencing and audit guardrails.
comparison.treatmentOutcome: With the licensed bundle, the remediation system restores verifier configuration in the right order, preserves session invariants, and emits the expected audit steps.
comparison.whyItHelped: The licensed bundle supplied specific rollback sequencing, issuer-mismatch diagnosis, and proof-backed validator patch guidance that the baseline system lacked.
comparison.businessImpact: Higher confidence incident recovery, faster safe rollback, and lower risk of shipping an auth fix that breaks live sessions.
comparison.interpretation: Licensed bundle improved the reader system's remediation performance on the target task.
checks: scored in basis points for simple comparability
